description:
A data-science colleague left you some messy CSV datasets in
    /home/user/projects/customer_churn/raw_data  and asked you to
    1) back them up, 2) create a compressed archive of the backup,  
    3) clean the files (remove blank lines, trim trailing whitespace,
       and drop duplicated data-rows **while keeping the header**),
    4) save the cleaned files in a parallel directory, and  
    5) register everything you did in one simple log file.

    Please do the entire job from the terminal using no more than
    about 5-10 shell commands.

    Directories that must exist **after you finish**  
      • /home/user/projects/customer_churn/backups/20230830  
      • /home/user/projects/customer_churn/archives  
      • /home/user/projects/customer_churn/cleaned_data  
      • /home/user/projects/customer_churn/logs  

    Files that must exist **after you finish**

      1. A verbatim copy of every original file inside  
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/backups/20230830/  
         (do not delete the originals in raw_data).

      2. A compressed gzip-tar archive named  
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/archives/raw_backup_20230830.tar.gz  
         The archive has to contain exactly the same files and *byte-for-byte*
         contents that are inside the freshly created backup directory.

      3. Two cleaned CSVs:
           /home/user/projects/customer_churn/cleaned_data/customers_2021.csv  
           /home/user/projects/customer_churn/cleaned_data/customers_2022.csv  

         Cleaning rules:
           • Preserve the first (header) line unchanged.  
           • For all following lines:
               – Strip trailing spaces or TABs.  
               – Discard any completely blank line.  
               – Discard rows that are perfect duplicates of an earlier row
                 *within the same file* (header is **not** counted when
                 checking duplicates).  
           • Keep the original column order and do not add or delete columns.

      4. One plain-text log file:
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/logs/cleanup_log.txt  

         The log must contain **exactly two lines**, one per processed file,
         in this comma-separated format (no leading or trailing spaces!):

             <filename>,<original_line_count>,<cleaned_line_count>,2023-08-30T00:00:00Z

         Example of the correct format (these numbers happen to be right for
         the provided data; do not change them):

             customers_2021.csv,6,4,2023-08-30T00:00:00Z
             customers_2022.csv,4,3,2023-08-30T00:00:00Z

         Where
           – <filename>                 is the base name only  
           – <original_line_count>      is the number of lines *including* the
                                         header before cleaning  
           – <cleaned_line_count>       is the number of lines *including* the
                                         header after cleaning  
           – The timestamp must be the literal string
                                         2023-08-30T00:00:00Z

    Verification details the automated grader will check
      • Existence of the four directories listed above.  
      • Byte-level equality between each file in backups/20230830 and its
        counterpart in raw_data.  
      • raw_backup_20230830.tar.gz unpacks to files that are also byte-identical
        to the backup copies.  
      • customers_2021.csv in cleaned_data has exactly 4 lines and its header
        matches the original header.  
      • customers_2022.csv in cleaned_data has exactly 3 lines and its header
        matches the original header.  
      • cleanup_log.txt exists, contains exactly the two lines shown above, and
        matches the format precisely (no extra blank lines).  

    Notes & constraints
      • Stay within ordinary user permissions; do not use sudo.  
      • You may assume the date 2023-08-30 is “today” for this task.  
      • Avoid hard-coding absolute paths inside the cleaned CSVs themselves
        (the CSV content must stay data-only).  
      • Feel free to print whatever status information you like to stdout,
        but do not write additional files outside the paths described above.

    When everything is done, the directory tree should look like this
      /home/user/projects/customer_churn/
        ├── archives/
        │     └── raw_backup_20230830.tar.gz
        ├── backups/
        │     └── 20230830/
        │           ├── customers_2021.csv
        │           └── customers_2022.csv
        ├── cleaned_data/
        │     ├── customers_2021.csv
        │     └── customers_2022.csv
        ├── logs/
        │     └── cleanup_log.txt
        └── raw_data/
              ├── customers_2021.csv
              └── customers_2022.csv

    Perform the task now.  Once you finish, the automated tests will run the
    checks listed above.
truth:
Initial file-system state BEFORE the agent starts

    Directory: /home/user/projects/customer_churn/raw_data
      customers_2021.csv
        ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │id,name,age,subscription\n                        │
        │1,Alice,30,Basic\n                                │
        │2,Bob,25,Premium\n                                │
        │3,Charlie,35,Basic\n                              │
        │2,Bob,25,Premium\n                                │
        │\n                                                │
        └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

      customers_2022.csv
        ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │id,name,age,subscription\n                        │
        │4,David,40,Premium\n                              │
        │5,Eva,28,Basic\n                                  │
        │4,David,40,Premium\n                              │
        └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    No other files or directories exist under
    /home/user/projects/customer_churn/  at task start.

    Expected FINAL state (values checked by the grader)

    1. Directories:
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/backups/20230830           (exists)
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/archives                   (exists)
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/cleaned_data               (exists)
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/logs                       (exists)

    2. Backup copies (byte-identical to originals):
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/backups/20230830/customers_2021.csv
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/backups/20230830/customers_2022.csv

    3. Archive file:
         /home/user/projects/customer_churn/archives/raw_backup_20230830.tar.gz
       • The archive extracts to exactly two files matching the backup copies,
         no extra members, no missing members.

    4. Cleaned CSVs:

       /home/user/projects/customer_churn/cleaned_data/customers_2021.csv  
         Exact content (4 lines, LF endings):
           id,name,age,subscription\n
           1,Alice,30,Basic\n
           2,Bob,25,Premium\n
           3,Charlie,35,Basic\n

       /home/user/projects/customer_churn/cleaned_data/customers_2022.csv  
         Exact content (3 lines, LF endings):
           id,name,age,subscription\n
           4,David,40,Premium\n
           5,Eva,28,Basic\n

    5. Log file:

       /home/user/projects/customer_churn/logs/cleanup_log.txt  
         Exact content (including final newline, total 2 lines):

           customers_2021.csv,6,4,2023-08-30T00:00:00Z\n
           customers_2022.csv,4,3,2023-08-30T00:00:00Z\n
